notes.race:amstel-gold-race-2026: Evenepoel's committed 42-km solo held to win by a single second over the defending champion in a two-up rematch — long-range racing settled by a nail-biting margin.
notes.race:copenhagen-sprint-2026: Rain and a late crash split the bunch, a stubborn breakaway was caught only inside the final kilometre, and Philipsen denied the Danish home hope in a chaotic wet reduced sprint.
notes.race:dwars-door-vlaanderen-2026: Van Aert's long-range move was reeled to a two-up finale and Ganna passed him on the line, but the reduced bunch never truly split — a clean if predictable rouleur-out-sprints-puncheur result.
notes.race:ixina-leeuw-oetingen-2026: Top favourite Wiebes won but did it the attacking way, jumping over the final climb for a two-up sprint instead of the expected bunch gallop — a nice twist on a low-key minor classic.
notes.race:muscat-classic-2026: A tidy Jayco numbers game trapped a lone Yates and Schmid took the two-up sprint, satisfying tactics but a favourite-team result on a minor season-opener.
notes.race:strade-bianche-2026: Pogačar's third Strade was a familiar Monte Sante Marie attack to a solo win, but 19-year-old Paul Seixas announcing himself with a minute-down second on debut over the brutal gravel made it compelling.
notes.stage:4-jours-de-dunkerque-2026-stage-4: A cobbled summit finish at Cassel split the bunch, an unsung Tesfatsion took the win while the unsupported leader chased to defend pink, tightening the GC to seven seconds.
notes.stage:boucles-de-la-mayenne-2026-stage-1: A routine bunch sprint won by Kooij with the GC frozen — a tune-up opener with little drama before the queen stage.
notes.stage:giro-d-italia-2026-stage-18: The steep Muro threatened to blow it apart but the sprinters survived and Magnier completed his hat-trick in a bunch finish with the GC untouched — good late skirmishing, expected outcome.
notes.stage:giro-ditalia-women-2026-stage-1: Wiebes won the sprint then was thrown off the entire race that evening for an underweight bike, handing Balsamo the stage and first pink, on a crash-marred opening day — extraordinary off-bike drama.
notes.stage:itzulia-basque-country-2026-stage-: Home favourite Aranburu took an uphill finish on Basque roads by four seconds, a solid emotional win but with no GC movement behind it.
notes.stage:paris-nice-2026-stage-7: Snow and rain neutralised the queen stage twice down to a 47-km reduced-bunch dash with no GC change — the marquee mountain day was effectively cancelled.
notes.stage:tirreno-adriatico-2026-stage-4: Long Apennine climbing trimmed the field and Van der Poel won his second reduced-group sprint — a strong day's racing but a favourite delivering as expected.
notes.stage:tour-de-hongrie-2026-stage-1: A textbook controlled bunch sprint won by Merlier exactly as scripted, decided only on bonus seconds.
notes.stage:tour-de-suisse-2026-stage-2: A six-man breakaway held off a hard-charging Pogačar by four seconds, Grégoire winning the uphill break sprint as the race leader's chase fell agonisingly short.
notes.stage:tour-down-under-2026-stage-0: A 3.6-km city prologue decided by one-second margins — low-stakes watching that mainly set the opening jersey.
notes.stage:tour-of-oman-2026-stage-4: A pure flat sprint stage won by Blikra with the GC frozen, a holding day before the Green Mountain decider.
notes.stage:tour-of-turkiye-2026-stage-2: Young leader Tom Crabbe doubled up with a second straight bunch sprint to extend his hold on the race lead — a decent fairy-tale-for-a-small-team angle but still a flat sprint day.
notes.stage:uae-tour-2026-stage-6: The Jebel Hafeet summit finish swung the overall as Del Toro attacked to take the stage and the race lead off Evenepoel — a decisive GC-carving mountain day.
notes.stage:volta-comunitat-valenciana-2026-st: A 17-km time trial won clearly by Evenepoel exactly as expected — a low-watchability race of truth against the clock.
notes.stage:vuelta-espana-femenina-2026-stage-: A wet, crash-hit opener that briefly caught Vos saw Rüegg pounce on the uphill drag to upset Kopecky by four seconds for the stage and first red jersey.
notes.race:amstel-gold-race-women-2026: 23-year-old Paula Blasi raids solo from 22 km out and holds off a minute over the favourites who marked each other for a breakthrough upset.
notes.race:copenhagen-sprint-women-2026: A controlled SD Worx lead-out delivers Wiebes to an expected bunch-sprint win on a flat day with little real jeopardy.
notes.race:dwars-door-vlaanderen-women-2026: Reusser edges Vollering in a Waregem photo finish after the leading duo nearly threw it away and Nooijen almost bridged late.
notes.race:koksijde-classic-2026: A flat coastal sprinters' classic goes exactly to script — break caught inside the last 1.6 km, Groenewegen wins the gallop.
notes.race:navarra-womens-classic-2026: Walls thin the field but it regroups for a sprint Cat Ferguson controls from the front for a tidy second Navarra title.
notes.race:strade-bianche-women-2026: Mechanicals and an off-course motorbike wipe out every favourite, and Chabbey wins the biggest race of her career in an explosive Piazza del Campo sprint.
notes.stage:4-jours-de-dunkerque-2026-stage-5: Red Bull 1-2 in the bunch sprint while the race leader survives a crash and a 4-km-to-go mechanical to clinch his first overall.
notes.stage:boucles-de-la-mayenne-2026-stage-2: A long queen-stage war of attrition ends in an uphill sprint as Cosnefroy wins and seizes yellow off the cracking leader.
notes.stage:giro-d-italia-2026-stage-19: Dolomite queen stage: Kuss reels in Ciccone and solos the win, Ciccone raids the Cima Coppi for the mountains jersey, and the GC podium reshuffles behind Gall's attack.
notes.stage:giro-ditalia-women-2026-stage-2: A routine sprinters' day with the break caught at 15 km to go and Balsamo winning the expected bunch gallop in pink.
notes.stage:itzulia-basque-country-2026-stage-: Brutal eight-climb queen stage settled by a two-up sprint as 19-year-old Seixas beats Lipowitz to seal a stage hat-trick and the overall.
notes.stage:paris-nice-2026-stage-8: Vingegaard and Martinez gap the field on the final climb for a two-up finish, but the overall was already decided.
notes.stage:tirreno-adriatico-2026-stage-5: Valgren bridges the break and solos the steep Marche muri stage while the GC men chase in behind.
notes.stage:tour-de-hongrie-2026-stage-2: Cosnefroy outfoxes the pure sprinters on the longest day, fracturing the finale to steal the stage and the lead.
notes.stage:tour-de-suisse-2026-stage-3: Narváez beats Meurisse in a storm-lashed two-up dash as the chasing peloton arrives just seconds too late.
notes.stage:tour-down-under-2026-stage-1: A hot Barossa bunch sprint with Lund Andresen edging Brennan — an opening-day gallop with no real drama.
notes.stage:tour-of-oman-2026-stage-5: On the savage Green Mountain summit Scaroni's last-km attack overturns a four-second deficit to seize stage and overall in one move as the leader cracks.
notes.stage:tour-of-turkiye-2026-stage-3: The first summit finish opens the GC as climber Iván Sosa attacks clear at Kıran to take the stage and the lead.
notes.stage:uae-tour-2026-stage-7: A pan-flat Abu Dhabi processional sprint with no GC consequence and barely any race to speak of.
notes.stage:volta-comunitat-valenciana-2026-st: After a chaotic downhill finale Andrew August times a daring late move to hold off the charging bunch for a maiden pro win.
notes.stage:vuelta-espana-femenina-2026-stage-: A reduced bunch sprint to Bossuyt, but the day turns on race leader Rüegg crashing out of the red jersey.
notes.race:antwerp-port-epic-ladies-2026: Femke Markus crashes early, chases back, then soloes 26 km on the gravel for a rare maiden-ish win and an SD Worx 1-2-3.
notes.race:cx-world-championships-2026-men: Van der Poel front-runs Hulst from lap one for a record eighth CX title on home sand — historic but never in doubt.
notes.race:e3-saxo-classic-2026: Van der Poel attacks the late bergs and soloes home, young Strand Hagenes chasing to within three seconds for a contested finish.
notes.race:kuurne-brussels-kuurne-2026: Wave after wave of climbs attacks all get caught and 20-year-old Brennan wins the reduced bunch sprint off a perfect Laporte lead-out.
notes.race:nokere-koerse-2026: Bunch comes back together and Philipsen wins the uphill cobbled drag exactly as the form sheet predicted.
notes.race:surf-coast-classic-men-2026: Road race cancelled the day before for bushfire and extreme-heat risk; only an unofficial stand-in criterium ran.
notes.stage:alula-tour-2026-stage-1: Desert crosswinds shatter the field into an 18-man front split and Milan wins the sprint while Christen crashes twice and loses time.
notes.stage:boucles-de-la-mayenne-2026-stage-3: Flat finale, Kooij doubles up in a routine bunch sprint and Cosnefroy rolls in to seal the overall.
notes.stage:giro-d-italia-2026-stage-2: A rain-soaked descent crash wipes out four UAE riders and their GC bid while unheralded Silva snatches the reduced-group sprint.
notes.stage:giro-ditalia-women-2026-stage-3: Vollering's Montenars attack drops the maglia rosa, but Balsamo claws back and wins the uphill sprint for three from three.
notes.stage:itzulia-basque-country-2026-stage-: Pure closing-day formality with the GC already sealed and the teenager Seixas just defending his lead home.
notes.stage:region-pays-de-la-loire-tour-2026-: Textbook flat sprinters' opener, break caught and Vernon takes the bunch gallop with no GC consequence.
notes.stage:tirreno-adriatico-2026-stage-6: Camerino summit finish where Del Toro's late attack splits the favourites and seals the GC by three seconds.
notes.stage:tour-de-hongrie-2026-stage-3: Hilly day stays together and Merlier survives the lumps to win a reduced sprint, his second of the week.
notes.stage:tour-de-suisse-2026-stage-4: A time trial decided by four hundredths of a second as Pogačar pips a storming Van der Poel by an invisible margin.
notes.stage:tour-down-under-2026-stage-2: The Corkscrew Road queen stage detonates the GC — Vine attacks for a UAE 1-2 and a minute-plus on the rest to take the race lead.
notes.stage:tour-of-slovenia-2026-stage-1: Strongest team controls the rolling finale and Pithie wins a reduced Red Bull-led sprint as expected.
notes.stage:tour-of-turkiye-2026-stage-4: Sprinter-friendly run-in after the climb, Aniołkowski wins the reduced bunch gallop with the GC frozen.
notes.stage:uae-tour-women-2026-stage-1: Congested desert opener, Wiebes finds a barrier gap and wins the bunch sprint precisely as favourite.
notes.stage:volta-comunitat-valenciana-2026-st: Teulada Moraira queen stage settles the GC as Evenepoel out-climbs the field and only Almeida limits losses to 24s.
notes.stage:vuelta-espana-femenina-2026-stage-: Kerbaol ambushes the flattest day with a 2km-out attack, holding off Kopecky to rob the sprinters.
notes.race:australian-road-nationals-men-2026: Jayco's own late chase reels in solo leader Durbridge inside the last km, leaving Conti rider Eddy to upset Plapp in a two-up sprint for the green-and-gold.
notes.race:cx-world-championships-2026-women: Veteran Brand grinds out a controlled solo to a second rainbow jersey for an all-Dutch sweep in a wide-open, champion-less field.
notes.race:eschborn-frankfurt-2026: Home favourite Zimmermann wins a reduced 12-rider sprint over Pidcock in a standard German May Day classic finish.
notes.race:la-fleche-wallonne-2026: 19-year-old debutant Seixas times the canonical 200m Mur de Huy explosion to perfection, with Schmid surging past Tulett for second in the final 50m.
notes.race:omloop-nieuwsblad-women-2026: A crash splits the bunch, Koch detonates the Muur, and two ex-TdFF winners Vollering and Niewiadoma go clear before Vollering takes the two-up sprint.
notes.race:surf-coast-classic-women-2026: Road race cancelled for bushfire risk; only an unofficial stand-in criterium was held.
notes.stage:alula-tour-2026-stage-2: Milan doubles up with a controlled textbook bunch sprint; crosswinds threatened but never broke the race.
notes.stage:giro-d-italia-2026-stage-1: Young Magnier takes a flat Black Sea bunch sprint for his maiden Grand Tour win and the first maglia rosa.
notes.stage:giro-d-italia-2026-stage-20: Vingegaard solos from 11km out for a dominant fifth stage win to seal the Giro, with only a brief late scrap for fourth behind.
notes.stage:giro-ditalia-women-2026-stage-4: Van der Breggen crushes the uphill TT by over a minute to seize pink — emphatic but a time-trial watch.
notes.stage:itzulia-women-2026-stage-1: A cold, wet, relentlessly climbing opener shatters the bunch and Bredewold wins a knife-edge five-up sprint in Zarautz.
notes.stage:region-pays-de-la-loire-tour-2026-: Vernon takes a second straight processional bunch sprint with no GC movement.
notes.stage:tirreno-adriatico-2026-stage-7: A closing-stage sprint formality with the overall already sealed the day before.
notes.stage:tour-de-hongrie-2026-stage-4: A rain-shortened queen stage sees Soderqvist solo clear on the wet Pecs circuit to win and flip the overall.
notes.stage:tour-de-suisse-women-2026-stage-1: De Vries and Dickson break clear on the climbs and survive repeated favourite attacks, De Vries taking her maiden pro win two-up.
notes.stage:tour-down-under-2026-stage-3: Welsford wins an expected reduced bunch sprint; a late crash inside 3km changes nothing on GC.
notes.stage:tour-of-slovenia-2026-stage-2: After a crash forces a mid-stage neutralisation, local sprinter Rajovic upsets race leader Pithie on the uphill finish in Ormoz.
notes.stage:tour-of-turkiye-2026-stage-5: Van Uden wins the long coastal stage in a routine sprint with no GC changes ahead of the queen stage.
notes.stage:uae-tour-women-2026-stage-2: Wiebes makes it two-from-two with a controlled bunch sprint and no echelon split.
notes.stage:volta-comunitat-valenciana-2026-st: An aggressive short final stage sees Garcia Pierna win from the breakaway while Evenepoel navigates a chaotic finale to seal the overall.
notes.stage:vuelta-espana-femenina-2026-stage-: SD Worx control a reduced sprint for a one-two as Kopecky finally converts her podiums into a win and takes red.
notes.race:australian-road-nationals-women-202: Home hero 20-year-old Coupland solos to the title past her own breakaway teammate for a fairy-tale Liv 1-2 and an elite/U23 double.
notes.race:cx-world-cup-benidorm-2026-men: Van der Poel rides everyone off his wheel from lap one in a foregone-conclusion solo, leaving only the Nys-vs-Orts home-soil scrap for second to enjoy.
notes.race:faun-drome-classic-2026: Grégoire and Jorgenson spring clear on the Mur d'Allex and settle it in a same-time two-up uphill sprint won by a bike length.
notes.race:la-fleche-wallonne-women-2026: Vollering attacks early and long on the Mur de Huy and just holds off Pieterse's ferocious same-time charge to the line.
notes.race:paris-roubaix-2026: Instant classic: Van Aert's first cobbled Monument off a perfect race, Pogacar cooked by three punctures, Van der Poel's four-peat buried in Arenberg, settled by a long velodrome sprint.
notes.race:the-gralloch-2026: A one-sided minute-plus men's solo for Postlberger paired with a two-second women's nailbiter as Schreurs becomes the first two-time winner.
notes.stage:alula-tour-2026-stage-3: The lone summit finish reshapes the GC with a late uphill lunge by Voisard after Christen's attack and a descent crash.
notes.stage:giro-d-italia-2026-stage-10: A flat ITT romp by Ganna for an INEOS 1-2; the GC is shaken but Eulalio clings to pink, classic skip-to-the-splits TT day.
notes.stage:giro-d-italia-2026-stage-21: Ceremonial Rome finale: Ganna's late show is caught, Milan wins the expected bunch sprint and Vingegaard rolls in to seal the Giro.
notes.stage:giro-ditalia-women-2026-stage-5: FDJ-SUEZ mountain masterclass shreds the field, Vollering attacks repeatedly and wins her first-ever Giro stage in a four-up sprint.
notes.stage:itzulia-women-2026-stage-2: Wlodarczyk and van Anrooij survive a late move by metres for the Pole's breakthrough maiden WorldTour win.
notes.stage:region-pays-de-la-loire-tour-2026-: 20-year-old L'Hote solos into Le Mans to flip the overall by 12 seconds on a final day that changed the lead twice.
notes.stage:tour-auvergne-rhone-alpes-2026-sta: Baudin outlasts the breakaway and holds off the closing peloton to solo into yellow on the opening hilly finish.
notes.stage:tour-de-hongrie-2026-stage-5: A routine bunch sprint hands Merlier a hat-trick while Soderqvist safely defends the overall, nothing decided on the road.
notes.stage:tour-de-suisse-women-2026-stage-2: Chaos-filled finale: Longo Borghini solos to stage and yellow as Reusser and Niewiadoma ride off course and Zigart crashes hard at the flamme rouge.
notes.stage:tour-down-under-2026-stage-4: 43C heat strips Willunga Hill from the route, turning the marquee mountain day into a substitute bunch sprint with the GC frozen.
notes.stage:tour-of-slovenia-2026-stage-3: Sprinter Pithie survives the climbs and chases down late attacks to take a sprint double and extend his lead before the queen stage.
notes.stage:tour-of-turkiye-2026-stage-6: Queen-stage summit finish: Bagatin wins from the break while Berwick distances Sosa to prise away the leader's jersey by five seconds.
notes.stage:uae-tour-women-2026-stage-3: A processional third straight flat day delivers an untouchable Wiebes another bunch-sprint win before the climbers' showdown.
notes.stage:vuelta-a-andalucia-ruta-del-sol-20: A lumpy opener never sheds the fast men and ends in a reduced bunch sprint won by Laporte into the first jersey.
notes.stage:vuelta-espana-femenina-2026-stage-: An SD Worx 1-2 sprint clinic with Kopecky leading out Bredewold, enlivened only by a late GC-leader crash that came to nothing.
notes.race:brabantse-pijl-2026: A late break caught at the flamme rouge sets up a reduced-group uphill drag where Foldager pounces for a breakthrough career win.
notes.race:cx-world-cup-benidorm-2026-women: Brand soloes clear at the end of lap two and rides uncontested to the win and a second straight World Cup title, the action confined to a scrap for the minor podium spots.
notes.race:figueira-champions-classic-2026: Home favourite Morgado defends his title in a two-up sprint after a textbook McNulty-built UAE setup, with a breakthrough podium for 21-year-old Pau Martí.
notes.race:liege-bastogne-liege-2026: Pogačar's canonical Redoute attack delivers an expected solo third Liège, but 19-year-old Seixas's lone chase to second, Bernal's comeback fifth and a cyclocrosser in fourth give it real depth.
notes.race:paris-roubaix-women-2026: Outnumbered two-to-one, Koch refuses to be controlled and edges Vos in a velodrome photo-sprint to deny Visma — and a grieving Vos an emotional win — in an instant classic.
notes.race:tour-down-under-womens-race-2026: A flat Barossa circuit stays together all day for a predictable bunch sprint, won by Coles-Lyster over the expected fast finishers.
notes.stage:alula-tour-2026-stage-4: A block headwind reels in a dangerous break and a lead-out-less desert sprint turns chaotic, Malucelli pouncing on a burnt-out Milan's wheel for a surprise win.
notes.stage:giro-d-italia-2026-stage-11: A big Cinque Terre breakaway day full of attacks and a descent crash resolves into a two-up sprint that Narváez wins for a Giro hat-trick.
notes.stage:giro-d-italia-2026-stage-3: A flat day with a token climb produces a routine bunch sprint, Magnier doubling up to keep pink.
notes.stage:giro-ditalia-women-2026-stage-6: A mid-stage Uno-X split briefly distances Vollering before a technical finale and a Brand-launched bunch sprint hands Balsamo a fourth stage win.
notes.stage:itzulia-women-2026-stage-3: The Mendizorrotz climb drops race leader Bredewold and throws the title into doubt before she descends and chases back to save the overall, as Włodarczyk doubles up.
notes.stage:setmana-volta-femenina-comunitat-v: Vollering delivers her familiar Barx blow and soloes the final 18.7 km to win alone and take the leader's jersey on day one.
notes.stage:tour-auvergne-rhone-alpes-2026-sta: A 234 km marathon breakaway day splinters repeatedly in the finale, Charmig timing it best to win clear from the move.
notes.stage:tour-de-romandie-2026-stage-0: A 3.2 km city-centre prologue won by TT specialist Godon, with Pogačar deliberately conservative.
notes.stage:tour-de-suisse-women-2026-stage-3: The break is caught for a hectic run-in and Bäckstedt blasts a dominant sprint, while a late crash knocks Dickson out of second overall.
notes.stage:tour-down-under-2026-stage-5: A kangaroo crosses the road and crashes the lead group, and from the reduced uphill sprint 21-year-old Brennan takes his maiden WorldTour win.
notes.stage:tour-of-slovenia-2026-stage-4: Red Bull lock out the queen stage with Lipowitz soloing over Vršič and Pellizzari descending back to cross the line hand-in-hand for a one-two and the GC lead.
notes.stage:tour-of-turkiye-2026-stage-7: A punchy Antalya circuit thins the bunch but resolves into a routine reduced sprint won by veteran Ballerini, with the GC unchanged.
notes.stage:uae-tour-women-2026-stage-4: Longo Borghini puts on a Jebel Hafeet masterclass, soloing inside the last 2.5 km to win the summit finish and seal a third overall title as expected.
notes.stage:vuelta-a-andalucia-ruta-del-sol-20: Romeo and Leknessund force a long-range two-man move that sticks, the Spanish champion dropping his companion in the final 2 km to win solo and take the jersey.
notes.stage:vuelta-espana-femenina-2026-stage-: A wet, crash-strewn day ends on the brutal Les Praeres wall where Van der Breggen rides everyone off her wheel to take red, setting up an 18-second Angliru cliffhanger.
notes.race:brussels-cycling-classic-2026: Climbs split the field and isolated Meeus before Red Bull dragged it back and led him out to edge Fretin and Girmay in a frantic but expected sprint.
notes.race:cx-world-cup-hoogerheide-2026-men: Van der Poel soloed away mid-race for a no-contest win — dominant but processional up front, with only a photo-finish chase sprint for second to watch.
notes.race:gent-wevelgem-2026: A Van der Poel/Van Aert/Segaert move on the Kemmelberg nearly stuck but was caught at 500m, gifting Philipsen the bunch sprint exactly as scripted.
notes.race:liege-bastogne-liege-women-2026: Vollering blew the race apart on La Redoute and soloed 35 km for a record third La Doyenne and Ardennes double — imperious but never in doubt after the attack.
notes.race:ronde-van-brugge-2026: A flat, processional sprinters' day with no selection — Groenewegen beat Philipsen in a textbook bunch gallop.
notes.race:tro-bro-leon-2026: A gravel-shattered classic where Visma's numbers game set up winter signing Fiorelli's perfectly-timed 3 km solo for a career-defining maiden win.
notes.stage:alula-tour-2026-stage-5: A summit-finish coup: Christen, back from two crashes, bridged then soloed the last 4 km to win the stage and snatch the overall as the leader cracked.
notes.stage:giro-d-italia-2026-stage-12: A nailed-on sprint day upended — TT man Segaert's late solo from 3.4 km held off the bunch for a surprise maiden Giro win after the climbs shelled the fast men.
notes.stage:giro-d-italia-2026-stage-4: The Cozzo Tunno climb shed the sprinters and Narváez took a punchy reduced-group sprint for UAE's first win two days after their crash disaster.
notes.stage:giro-ditalia-women-2026-stage-7: Longo Borghini lit up the Pietragavina to forge the winning move and 20-year-old Gery came over the top for a maiden Grand Tour win, while pink survived a heavy crash.
notes.stage:paris-nice-2026-stage-1: A controlled reduced-bunch sprint won by Lamperti for his first WorldTour win — pleasant upset but a tame opener.
notes.stage:setmana-volta-femenina-comunitat-v: Climbs early, long flat run-in, predictable bunch sprint won by 19-year-old Ferguson with the GC untouched.
notes.stage:tour-auvergne-rhone-alpes-2026-sta: A team time trial — Visma edged INEOS by nine seconds and reshuffled the GC, but low-drama viewing.
notes.stage:tour-de-romandie-2026-stage-1: Pogačar won a reduced-bunch kick over Lipowitz and Martinez to take yellow — sharp finale but the expected favourite delivering as expected.
notes.stage:tour-de-suisse-women-2026-stage-4: Reusser's home-roads ITT masterclass flipped the race lead, but a time trial setting up a 10-second showdown rather than thrilling in itself.
notes.stage:tour-feminin-international-des-pyr: Kvasničková's long solo built three minutes before being caught at 11 km, setting up Alzini's first win in three years from the bunch sprint.
notes.stage:tour-of-the-alps-2026-stage-1: A tactical thriller: INEOS burned their train and Arensman's late solo was caught in the final metres before unheralded Dati popped out of Pidcock's slipstream for a breakthrough win.
notes.stage:tour-of-turkiye-2026-stage-8: A flat finale bunch sprint, Crabbe completing a hat-trick while the GC leader rode safely home — tidy but uneventful.
notes.stage:volta-ao-algarve-2026-stage-1: A flat coastal opener decided by an expected bunch sprint, Magnier edging Meeus with the GC men all together.
notes.stage:vuelta-a-andalucia-ruta-del-sol-20: An explosive uphill drag to Lopera produced a punchy three-up sprint won by Fretin, but no GC shake-up.
notes.stage:vuelta-espana-femenina-2026-stage-: GC carnage on the brutal Angliru — Blasi cracked race leader Van der Breggen on the 20% ramps to steal the overall by 24 seconds, while Stiasny stormed past for a stunning solo stage win.
notes.race:cadel-evans-great-ocean-road-race-2: A routine reduced-bunch sprint with a clean expected order — Lund Andresen over Brennan — and no real drama on a standard Geelong day.
notes.race:cx-world-cup-hoogerheide-2026-women: Pieterse whittled the group down and soloed the final lap for a World Cup-finale weekend double, but a front-runner winning as expected with tight chase gaps keeps it solid rather than special.
notes.race:gent-wevelgem-women-2026: Wiebes detonated the race herself on the final Kemmelberg, forced a select five clear, then won a very long sprint from the front by a wheel from 20-year-old Moors — a hat-trick statement on a brutal gravel-and-cobbles finale.
notes.race:marly-grav-race-2026: Van Aert soloed 22 km on his post-Roubaix gravel return while world champ Wiebes won the women's sprint — fun star turns, but both wins came as the strongest riders did the expected thing.
notes.race:ronde-van-brugge-women-2026: Crosswinds, hail and echelons turned a sprinters' day into a survival test, and 19-year-old neo-pro Carys Lloyd stunned the field with a breakthrough long sprint while defending champ Wiebes was boxed out to 9th.
notes.race:trofeo-laigueglia-2026: Buitrago used Bahrain's numbers in the trio to launch a clean 11 km solo for his first win in over a year, after Vervaeke's long break and a Colla Micheri explosion — aggressive but a fairly standard puncheur finale.
notes.stage:baloise-belgium-tour-2026-stage-1: An ordinary opening-day bunch sprint won by Girmay over Merlier, the break caught as expected with no drama beyond the leader's jersey on bonuses.
notes.stage:giro-d-italia-2026-stage-13: A 15-man break never chased, then Bettiol exploded over Ungiasca past a solo Leknessund and bombed the technical descent for a clean solo Giro win to Verbania — a great breakaway day, if GC-neutral.
notes.stage:giro-d-italia-2026-stage-5: Rain-chaos hilly stage to Potenza settled by a small front group, with Arrieta winning and Eulálio into pink — eventful conditions but thinly documented racing.
notes.stage:giro-ditalia-women-2026-stage-8: An ice-block re-route slashed the queen stage to a makeshift Finestre gravel finish where Vollering attacked relentlessly and won by a second over an isolated van der Breggen, with four riders inside a second in fog and chaos — unmissable.
notes.stage:paris-nice-2026-stage-2: A textbook lead-out bunch sprint won by Kanter with no GC consequence — skip-to-the-finale stuff.
notes.stage:setmana-volta-femenina-comunitat-v: The GC-deciding queen stage was cancelled on safety grounds after an orange wind warning — no racing took place.
notes.stage:tour-auvergne-rhone-alpes-2026-sta: The break held to the line despite shrinking under 30 seconds, with Simmons winning a small sprint over Fisher-Black — a tense breakaway-vs-peloton finish but GC-neutral.
notes.stage:tour-de-romandie-2026-stage-2: Pogačar doubled up with a reduced-group sprint win exactly as expected, tightening his grip on the GC — dominant favourite, little suspense.
notes.stage:tour-de-wallonie-2026-stage-1: Repeated cobbled Thuin walls shredded the bunch before Meeus took a reduced sprint to open in yellow — a solid hard-finisher's day with real selection but a familiar outcome.
notes.stage:tour-feminin-international-des-pyr: Blasi attacked on the Tourmalet over 10 km from the top and soloed to a near-two-minute win, effectively deciding the race and forming a UAE 1-2 — a committed long-range mountain raid.
notes.stage:tour-of-the-alps-2026-stage-2: A brutal Val Martello summit finish where home rider Pellizzari outkicked Arensman to take the stage and jersey, launching his maiden GC win with Bernal in the lead group — real selection and emotion.
notes.stage:uae-tour-2026-stage-1: Del Toro launched inside the final kilometre on the draggy Liwa Palace uphill to win a wind-shortened opener and take the jersey — a clean punchy finale but a predictable result.
notes.stage:volta-ao-algarve-2026-stage-2: 19-year-old Seixas outsprinted Ayuso atop Fóia for a marquee summit win while Ayuso took yellow on bonuses — a genuine GC test with a fresh young winner, but a measured finale.
notes.stage:vuelta-a-andalucia-ruta-del-sol-20: Late Wellens-and-co attacks were caught and 20-year-old Crabbe took a clean bunch-sprint win, with the Golden Kilometre bonuses reshuffling the GC chase — lively but ultimately a sprint day.
notes.stage:women-tour-down-under-2026-stage-1: Wollaston judged the chase perfectly to run down long-range solo attacker Vigilia inside the final 300 m on Willunga Hill for the season-opening win and ochre jersey — a genuinely tense catch-or-not finish.
notes.race:cadel-evans-great-ocean-road-race-w: Challambra thinned it to a dozen and Wollaston won the reduced bunch sprint for a back-to-back title, but the long-range solo was caught and the finale was controlled.
notes.race:cx-world-cup-maasmechelen-2026-men: Van der Poel's 50th World Cup win and an Alpecin 1-2-3 podium sweep, with two punctures the only thing keeping it from being a total procession.
notes.race:gp-de-denain-2026: Segaert sat patiently behind Hagenes for 10 km then launched a flying late jump at 2.5 km to steal a cobbled solo win by metres over the swarming peloton.
notes.race:milano-sanremo-2026: Pogačar crashed at 32 km, remounted, attacked the Cipressa, dropped Van der Poel on the Poggio and beat Pidcock by centimetres on Via Roma for the Monument he'd needed.
notes.race:ronde-van-vlaanderen-2026: Pogačar attacked on the Oude Kwaremont, dropped four-time winner Van der Poel on his own climb and soloed 34 seconds clear to a back-to-back Ronde on brutal bergs.
notes.race:vuelta-cv-feminas-2026: Lippert timed a late solo with 3 km to go and just held off the chasers by six seconds for an emotional Spanish win as Movistar locked out the front.
notes.stage:baloise-belgium-tour-2026-stage-2: A controlled flat coastal day ending in the expected bunch sprint, Merlier beating Kooij and Philipsen to take the lead on bonuses.
notes.stage:giro-d-italia-2026-stage-14: Vingegaard soloed to a third summit win and ripped the maglia rosa off Eulálio, who cracked and shipped 2:26 as the entire Giro GC was overturned in one climb.
notes.stage:giro-d-italia-2026-stage-6: A fast coastal day ended in a technical cobbled sprint on Naples' Piazza del Plebiscito, Ballerini timing it best ahead of Stuyven and Magnier.
notes.stage:giro-ditalia-women-2026-stage-9: A final-day ambush: Vollering overturned a 0:49 deficit, dropped van der Breggen on the last climb, bridged across and grabbed a bonus to steal the Giro and complete the Triple Crown, with Longo Borghini winning the emotional four-up sprint.
notes.stage:paris-nice-2026-stage-3: A flat team time trial decided by two seconds — tactically tidy GC setup but low-drama to watch.
notes.stage:setmana-volta-femenina-comunitat-v: Vollering forced the selection over the climb and out-sprinted the five-rider front group to seal a wire-to-wire overall and the double.
notes.stage:tour-auvergne-rhone-alpes-2026-sta: The break was caught with 12 km left and Van Aert won the expected bunch sprint with no GC change.
notes.stage:tour-de-romandie-2026-stage-3: A hilly Orbe circuit over the Col du Mollendruz came back together for a reduced-bunch sprint, Godon taking his second win of the week.
notes.stage:tour-de-wallonie-2026-stage-2: A rain-scrambled finale upended the sprinters as unheralded Ben Oliver stunned Meeus and De Lie for the first-ever European win of Hincapie's small American team and the race lead.
notes.stage:tour-feminin-international-des-pyr: A 10-rider break stayed clear and Jansen crashed alone inside the final kilometre but remounted to still win the stage, with the overall already settled behind.
notes.stage:tour-of-the-alps-2026-stage-3: A 30-rider pile-up neutralised the start and a crash-hit, attack-filled finale exploded in the final km, with Pidcock back from injury timing the late sprint to win in Arco.
notes.stage:uae-tour-2026-stage-2: A pan-flat ITT dominated by Evenepoel; decisive for GC but a low-drama watch.
notes.stage:volta-ao-algarve-2026-stage-3: A flat ITT routinely won by Ganna at 53.5 km/h; the only intrigue was climber Ayuso defending yellow by limiting his loss.
notes.stage:vuelta-a-andalucia-ruta-del-sol-20: Pidcock attacked on the final climb, dropped everyone and descended solo to the win while Romeo clung to the overall by seven seconds in a tight GC finale.
notes.stage:women-tour-down-under-2026-stage-2: An attack-filled day in fierce heat boiled down to an uphill sprint where Wollaston came from deeper to pip Rüegg in the final metres for a second straight win.
notes.race:circuit-franco-belge-2026: Attack-laden finale on the Mont-de-l'Enclus climbs but it came back to a reduced uphill sprint, where Corbin Strong launched first and held off Foldager and Magnier for his first win of 2026.
notes.race:cx-world-cup-maasmechelen-2026-wome: Puck Pieterse soloed clear with a lap-four move for a fairly clean Fenix one-two over teammate Alvarado, with the only late drama being Fouquenet's puncture costing her second.
notes.race:gp-du-morbihan-2026: Standard control-and-reduced-uphill-sprint day on the Côte de Cadoudal where favourite Benoît Cosnefroy timed it perfectly for a fourth career win, despite Decathlon putting two on the podium.
notes.race:milano-sanremo-donne-2026: A Cipressa-descent crash wiped out aggressors Niewiadoma and Le Court, Pieterse's Poggio surge forged a winning five, and Lotte Kopecky out-read everyone for a Monument in a +8s nail-biter on the Via Roma.
notes.race:ronde-van-vlaanderen-women-2026: Crash carnage broke Reusser's and Le Court's bones before the Koppenberg, then Demi Vollering soloed 18 km from the Oude Kwaremont to finally claim her first Ronde, 42s clear with PFP edging Pieterse for second.
notes.stage:4-jours-de-dunkerque-2026-stage-1: Bunch arrived together at the twice-climbed Laon uphill finish where Laurence Pithie nailed the punchy sprint over Coquard and Askey to take the first pink jersey on a tiny four-second margin.
notes.stage:baloise-belgium-tour-2026-stage-3: The savage Muur van Durbuy queen stage dropped the sprinters and produced an uphill duel where Alex Aranburu came over the top of Lewis Askey in the final metres to win and seize the overall lead.
notes.stage:giro-d-italia-2026-stage-15: A four-man break stunned a complacent peloton on a pancake-flat run into Milan, with Fredrik Dversnes outkicking three Italians for Uno-X's first-ever Giro win as the sprinters arrived nine seconds too late.
notes.stage:giro-d-italia-2026-stage-7: Vingegaard soloed the final 7 km of the Blockhaus queen stage for his first non-Tour Grand Tour win, dropping the GC field, though breakaway-man Eulálio clung to the maglia rosa with his lead only halved.
notes.stage:itzulia-basque-country-2026-stage-: A hilly opening ITT, but a memorable one: 19-year-old Paul Seixas beat every billed favourite by 23s to become the youngest WorldTour stage-race leader of the year as Ayuso shipped 1:16.
notes.stage:paris-nice-2026-stage-4: Atrocious weather and a mass crash that ended Ayuso's race set the chaos, before Vingegaard attacked inside the final kilometre at Uchon to ride 42s clear and grab yellow on the spring's wildest GC day.
notes.stage:tirreno-adriatico-2026-stage-1: A pan-flat opening time trial that Filippo Ganna predictably dominated, the only flavour being teammate Arensman's surprise second for an INEOS one-two.
notes.stage:tour-auvergne-rhone-alpes-2026-sta: An early echelon split caught the yellow jersey and every GC favourite on the wrong side, blowing the race apart; Van Gils won from the front group and Luke Tuckwell raided into the overall lead.
notes.stage:tour-de-romandie-2026-stage-4: Pogačar attacked late on the Jaunpass and soloed the descent to a third stage win of the week, 14s over Lipowitz — aggressive but a thoroughly expected dominant-favourite result.
notes.stage:tour-de-wallonie-2026-stage-3: A mass crash inside the final kilometre shattered the bunch on a sprinters' day, and Pithie sprinted clear of the survivors on the uphill drag with Heiduk taking the race lead.
notes.stage:tour-of-oman-2026-stage-1: A textbook flat opener decided by an expected bunch sprint, Sebastián Molano edging Gaviria and Thijssen to take the first leader's jersey on bonuses.
notes.stage:tour-of-the-alps-2026-stage-4: Lennart Jasch soloed from the break for a surprise career-best win in Trento while the GC men marked each other, leaving four seconds covering the top three into the final day.
notes.stage:uae-tour-2026-stage-3: Sparse coverage of an intermediate stage with no documented podium or decisive racing, overshadowed by the week's bigger ITT and summit-finish days.
notes.stage:volta-ao-algarve-2026-stage-4: A routine reduced bunch sprint into Lagos won, as expected, by Paul Magnier over Meeus for his second of the week, with the GC frozen ahead of the mountains.
notes.stage:vuelta-a-burgos-feminas-2026-stage: A controlled, by-the-numbers SD Worx lead-out delivered an untouchable Lorena Wiebes to a clear-margin sprint win and the first leader's jersey — exactly as billed.
notes.stage:women-tour-down-under-2026-stage-3: Over the double Corkscrew the leader was dropped and Rüegg, outnumbered three-to-one by UAE, marked every attack and out-sprinted the trio in Campbelltown to seal back-to-back titles by one second.
notes.race:clasica-de-almeria-2026: Crash-marred chaotic finale but ends in an expected Girmay bunch sprint off a four-man leadout.
notes.race:cx-world-cup-zonhoven-2026-men: Van der Poel rides an untouchable ninth-straight on frozen sand, but Nys flipping over the fencing into the crowd is the real spectacle.
notes.race:gravel-one-fifty-2026: Flat, bunched Drenthe gravel decided by a near photo-finish group sprint among lesser-known names with no real storyline.
notes.race:milano-torino-2026: Relentlessly aggressive Superga finale with repeated attacks and late lead changes, settled by Pidcock's perfectly-timed 600m kick on debut.
notes.race:scheldeprijs-2026: Late crashes carve up the field but Merlier still surges from the wheels for an expected third straight pure-sprint win.
notes.stage:4-jours-de-dunkerque-2026-stage-2: Long-range five-man break beats the chasing sprint teams, with minor-team Papon outkicking his companions for an upset win.
notes.stage:baloise-belgium-tour-2026-stage-4: Routine controlled bunch sprint among the expected fast men, decided only by Kooij's late kick.
notes.stage:giro-d-italia-2026-stage-16: Vingegaard's dominant 6.5km solo for a fourth Giro mountain win, with a tight three-man scrap igniting behind for the podium.
notes.stage:giro-d-italia-2026-stage-8: Attacking-all-day hilly stage on the Marche muri, Narvaez soloing off the Capodarco to deny an Uno-X 2-3.
notes.stage:itzulia-basque-country-2026-stage-: Seixas drops every GC favourite on the queen-stage summit to win by 1:23 and effectively end the race early.
notes.stage:paris-nice-2026-stage-5: Vingegaard's 20km solo for a two-minute race-deciding win, impressive but a one-sided demolition by the strongest man.
notes.stage:tirreno-adriatico-2026-stage-2: Van der Poel wins a wet-gravel reduced-group sprint into San Gimignano, a solid Strade Bianche dress rehearsal.
notes.stage:tour-auvergne-rhone-alpes-2026-sta: Del Toro storms the Grand Colombier while Seixas crashes and chases back 70km, dramatically tightening the GC before the finale.
notes.stage:tour-de-romandie-2026-stage-5: Pogacar solos for a fourth stage win and the overall exactly as expected, with no one able to follow.
notes.stage:tour-de-wallonie-2026-stage-4: De Lie launches inside the final 500m to overhaul Sheehan for an emotional last-gasp home win with the GC wafer-thin.
notes.stage:tour-of-oman-2026-stage-2: Young Frenchman Veistroffer takes a breakthrough small-group win at the Yitti Hills and grabs the overall lead.
notes.stage:tour-of-the-alps-2026-stage-5: Pellizzari defends a four-second lead, attacks to drop Bernal and Arensman, and solos to a maiden career GC title as the podium reshuffles.
notes.stage:uae-tour-2026-stage-4: Textbook Lidl-Trek leadout delivers an expected Jonathan Milan bunch-sprint win after the break is caught late.
notes.stage:volta-ao-algarve-2026-stage-5: Tense GC decider with a Vauquelin/Lipowitz move briefly threatening yellow before Ayuso wins the uphill sprint to seal the overall.
notes.stage:vuelta-a-burgos-feminas-2026-stage: A second straight controlled SD Worx leadout delivers an untouchable Wiebes for the expected bunch-sprint win.
notes.race:classique-dunkerque-2026: Crosswinds and rain shattered the bunch and Shmidt soloed clear inside 14km for a maiden pro win and his rebranded team's first-ever victory.
notes.race:cx-world-cup-zonhoven-2026-women: A survival race on snow and ice where Alvarado crashed early then pounced when leader Brand slid into the fencing on the final laps to win again at Zonhoven.
notes.race:highlands-gravel-classic-2026: Solo-flavoured wins for Mock and a repeat champion Rinehart as the Ozark climbs split the field, but largely a privateer time-gap grind with little drama detail.
notes.race:monaco-gravel-race-2026: Bardet and Wright both soloed away on the Mercantour climbs for emphatic but uncontested wins with huge gaps.
notes.race:seven-nannup-2026: Johnston outfoxed a three-up finale to ride away by seven seconds while Cromwell time-trialled to a solo third SEVEN title on the future Worlds course.
notes.stage:4-jours-de-dunkerque-2026-stage-3: A cobbled queen stage over the Paris-Roubaix pavé where Tiller soloed clear and the leader Pithie answered on the Arenberg to defend pink.
notes.stage:boucles-de-la-mayenne-2026-stage-0: A 5.4km prologue ITT decided by a single second — tight but a short time trial with little to watch.
notes.stage:giro-d-italia-2026-stage-17: A chaotic all-day breakaway war with Cavagna's long solo and constant reshuffling, settled when 34-year-old Valgren timed a flamme-rouge kick for an emotional first Grand Tour win.
notes.stage:giro-d-italia-2026-stage-9: A brutal summit-finish duel where Ciccone soloed off the break and Vingegaard shadowed Gall up the savage Corno ramps before kicking clear after the flamme rouge for a statement GC win.
notes.stage:itzulia-basque-country-2026-stage-: Laurance gapped the favourites on the uphill drag into Basauri to hang on by two seconds, but the GC barely moved.
notes.stage:paris-nice-2026-stage-6: Tejada bridged off the early break and held a hilly run to Apt by six seconds over a chasing group with no GC consequence.
notes.stage:tirreno-adriatico-2026-stage-3: A clean, controlled bunch sprint won by Lund Andresen with the expected fast men behind — the dull-sprint archetype.
notes.stage:tour-auvergne-rhone-alpes-2026-sta: A queen-stage GC heist: del Toro bridged to the break and soloed clear on the final climb to overturn Tuckwell's yellow and win the race, with Seixas abandoning.
notes.stage:tour-de-suisse-2026-stage-1: Pogačar detonated the opener with an outrageous 71km solo raid, dropping the whole field and all but sealing GC on day one.
notes.stage:tour-de-wallonie-2026-stage-5: An Ardennes finale over La Redoute with bonus-second GC chess, late attacks and a 50m last-gasp surge from Oliver to snatch the stage and the overall by two seconds.
notes.stage:tour-of-oman-2026-stage-3: The first GC selection on an uphill finish, Schmid edging Scaroni to take the lead by four seconds — solid but a modest two-man margin.
notes.stage:tour-of-turkiye-2026-stage-1: A routine reeled-in-break bunch sprint, though neo-pro Crabbe's breakthrough win and first jersey adds a little spark.
notes.stage:uae-tour-2026-stage-5: A pan-flat Dubai bunch sprint won exactly as expected by Milan with his lead-out train — skip to the finale.
notes.stage:volta-comunitat-valenciana-2026-st: A standard reeled-in-break bunch sprint for Girmay on his new-team debut, GC effectively untouched.
notes.stage:vuelta-a-burgos-feminas-2026-stage: The climbs broke the sprint pattern and SD Worx played a different card with Bredewold attacking late from a reduced group for a hat-trick, tightening the GC.
